violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (lemons) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Lemons disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025

A wet wiping cloth was observed draped over the hand-washing sink in the server area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025
Lemon whip was observed with date mark exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025
The lids to the prep units were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025
A leak was observed at the faucet of the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025
The fan guards in the walk-in were observed with dust and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 12/26/2025
Menus are lacking allergy statement. English Food Allergies What You Need to Know New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service called during inspection. The kitchen dishwasher will be used until the bar dishwasher has been serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Cheese was observed held at degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings in the maintenance hallway leading to the walk in cooler. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Orkin PCS weekly. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
Water observed in stacked shaker cups at the bar. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
Ice wands observed stored inside an unclean chest freezer. Equipment and utensils must be protected from contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the floor mixer have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The facility is lacking a mop sink during inspection. A new mop sink was purchased, and is on site, and will be installed tonight per PIC. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The floors in the maintenance hallway, in the bar, and beneath equipment in the cooking area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024
